title: Calls, parameters and returns deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Calls, parameters and returns; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Calls, parameters and returns: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Parameters permanently replace the caller's variable names, so a function has no separate local frame.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Trace caller and callee frames separately: arguments produce values, parameters are local bindings, and only declared returned values or effects cross the boundary.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Trace caller and callee frames separately: arguments produce values, parameters are local bindings, and only declared returned values or effects cross the boundary.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Calls, parameters and returns instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Trace caller and callee frames separately: arguments produce values, parameters are local bindings, and only declared returned values or effects cross the boundary.
Parameters permanently replace the caller's variable names, so a function has no separate local frame.

answer.explanation: Trace caller and callee frames separately: arguments produce values, parameters are local bindings, and only declared returned values or effects cross the boundary.
